title: Truce
content:
Far inland here Death's pinions mocked the roar
     Of English seas;
 We sleep to wake no more,
     Hushed, and at ease;
 Till sound a trump, shore on to echoing shore,
 Rouse from a peace, unwonted then to war,
     Us and our enemies.
meaning:
Walter De La Mare's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Truce"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
